title: sorbus cretica var. hungarica
intro: Sorbus cretica var. hungarica, also known as Hungarian Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Central Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing antioxidant protection. The herbal preparations of Sorbus cretica var. hungarica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
